title: CSS Filters and Visual Effects Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the essential properties for applying graphical filters and post-processing effects directly in your browser using CSS.

questions.question: Which CSS property is specifically used to apply effects like blurring, color shifting, or brightness adjustment to an element?
Which filter value is used to create a Gaussian blur effect in CSS?
Which of these is the most modern and recommended way to build a 'frosted glass' (glassmorphism) effect in a professional-grade web application?
What is the primary danger of applying too many complex filters (like large blurs) to every element on a page?
options: The site will load too fast.
Filters can be very expensive (slow) for the browser to render, especially on mobile devices.
Search engines will block the site.
The browser will automatically translate the CSS into JavaScript.

questions.question: Which filter value is specifically used to turn an image into a 'old-fashioned' black and white or sepia tone?
